title: Frequently Asked Questions
general_a1: MTGx is a coordination platform for Magic: The Gathering. It helps players find games and lets organizers and clubs fill their tables. It works as a web app on both mobile and desktop — sign in with one tap via Google. Visit mtgx.app.
general_q2: How is this better than WhatsApp groups?
general_a2: WhatsApp is great for chatting, but poor for coordination. MTGx has format and city filters, event registration with seat tracking, an automatic waitlist, and push notifications. No need to scroll through 100 messages to see who's coming — everything is visible on the event card.
general_q3: Is this a replacement for WhatsApp?
general_a3: No. MTGx and WhatsApp work together. Create an event in MTGx, share the link in your WhatsApp group. People register through MTGx (RSVP, waitlist, notifications), while conversation stays in WhatsApp.
general_a4: For now, yes. Focusing on Israel is a deliberate decision — a working product in one region is better than a thin spread worldwide. Expansion is planned.
general_a5: Free for players, forever. No trial period, no hidden charges. Creating events for organizers is also free. Club pages are free. A Pro subscription for clubs with advanced analytics is planned for the future.
general_a6: MTGx was built by an MTG community enthusiast in Israel. It is not a venture-backed startup — the product grows organically based on community needs.
general_q7: What happens to my data? Do you sell it?
general_a7: No. MTGx does not sell or share your data. Only the minimum is stored: name, avatar, city, formats, and schedule. No passwords are stored — login is via Google.
getting_started_a1: Open mtgx.app in your browser and tap "Sign in with Google". After signing in, complete the onboarding form: choose your city, formats, and availability schedule. You will then land in the events feed.
getting_started_q2: Do I have to fill in everything during registration?
getting_started_a2: Your city and at least one format are required — without them, the events feed will be empty. Schedule, WhatsApp number, and car availability can be added later in the Profile section.
getting_started_q3: I skipped onboarding and now the feed is empty. What do I do?
getting_started_a3: Go to Profile and fill in your city and formats. Without this information the system does not know which events to show you. The feed will refresh once you save your details.
getting_started_q4: Why only Google login? I don't have a Google account.
getting_started_a4: Google login is more secure — no password to invent or remember. If you do not have a Google account, create one for free at accounts.google.com — it takes about 2 minutes.
getting_started_q5: Is signing in with Google safe?
getting_started_a5: Yes. MTGx only receives your name, email, and avatar. There is no access to your Gmail, contacts, or any other Google services. You can revoke access at any time in your Google account settings.
getting_started_q6: How do I change my avatar?
getting_started_a6: Go to Profile, tap your avatar, and upload a photo. You can also keep the photo from your Google account.
getting_started_q7: How do I switch the language?
getting_started_a7: Open Settings (the gear icon) and use the language toggle: EN / RU / HE. The language changes instantly.
getting_started_q8: How do I enable dark mode?
getting_started_a8: The theme toggle button is located in the top bar of the application.
events_q1: How do I find an event nearby?
events_a1: The main screen shows an events feed already filtered by your city. Format filters are available at the top. If nothing shows up, try clearing the filters or check your city in your profile.
events_q2: The events feed is empty — is the platform down?
events_a2: Most likely there is not much activity in your city yet. Try expanding the filters or selecting "All cities". Subscribe to a format and you will get a notification when a matching event appears.
events_q3: How do I register for an event?
events_a3: Open the event card and press the RSVP button. Three options are available: "Going", "Maybe", and "Not going". If there are no seats left, a "Join waitlist" button will appear.
events_q4: I RSVPed but my plans changed. How do I cancel?
events_a4: Open the event and change your RSVP to "Not going". The first person on the waitlist will automatically receive the seat and a push notification.
events_q5: I am on the waitlist — how will I know when a spot opens up?
events_a5: You will receive a push notification. Make sure you have allowed notifications (see the Notifications section).
events_q6: What does the fill circle on the event card mean?
events_a6: It is a capacity indicator showing how many seats are taken out of the maximum. It lets you quickly see whether it is worth registering.
lfg_a1: LFG (Looking For Game) is a way to tell everyone in your city "I am free and want to play". Pick a format and a time window — your signal is visible to all players in your city. When 3+ people respond, a prompt to create a meetup appears. It replaces the "who's playing today?" thread in WhatsApp.
lfg_q2: How do I send an LFG signal?
lfg_a2: Find the LFG icon in the bottom navigation bar. Select a format and duration — your signal will appear in the feed for all players in your city and will disappear automatically when the time expires.
lfg_q3: I sent an LFG signal and nobody responded. What is wrong?
lfg_a3: Most likely few players are online right now — that is normal in a smaller community. Try in the evening or on weekends, and consider broadening your format selection. Subscribing to other players in your city will alert you when they post a signal.
lfg_q4: Too many people responded — what do I do?
lfg_a4: That is a good problem! Split into two tables or arrange an extra session. The important thing is not to leave people who responded without an answer.
organizer_q1: How do I create an event?
organizer_a1: Tap the "+" button in the bottom navigation to open the creation form. Two types are available: "Tournament" (full form with name, venue, entry fee, and description) and "Quick Game" (format, time, and place in about 10 seconds — disappears after 24 hours).
organizer_q2: What is the difference between a tournament and a quick game?
organizer_a2: A tournament is for planned events: full form, registration, optional entry fee. A quick game is for spontaneous meetups: minimal details, lives for 24 hours.
organizer_q3: I run the same event every week. Do I have to start from scratch each time?
organizer_a3: No. Create it once, then clone it — all fields are pre-filled and you only change the date. Templates for recurring events are also available.
organizer_q4: How do I cancel an event?
organizer_a4: On the event page, press the "Cancel" button. All attendees (going / maybe / waitlist) will automatically receive a push notification about the cancellation.
organizer_q5: How do I message all participants?
organizer_a5: On the event management page, use "Message participants". All registered attendees will receive a push notification. Handy for "starting late", "location changed", or "need one more player".
organizer_q6: How do I know who will actually show up?
organizer_a6: Each player has a reliability rating visible only to organizers, showing how often they show up after confirming. QR Check-in at the door records actual attendance.
organizer_a7: The organizer displays a QR code on their phone and players scan it to confirm their arrival. This data is used to calculate the reliability rating.
organizer_q8: I already run tournaments through Challonge/Melee. Does MTGx replace them?
organizer_a8: No. MTGx does not manage brackets or track standings. MTGx handles the pre-tournament stage: gathering players, RSVP, reminders, and LFG. Use your usual tools for the tournament itself.
organizer_q9: How do I become an organizer?
organizer_a9: The organizer role is assigned by an administrator. Use the feedback button in the app and tell us what events you plan to run.
mtg_q1: How does the deck power scale (Commander Brackets) work?
mtg_a1: When RSVPing to a Commander event you indicate a power level from 1 to 5 using the MTGx scale: 1 is an unmodified precon, 3 is an optimized casual deck, and 5 is cEDH. Organizers see the distribution and can balance tables before the game starts.
mtg_q2: Is indicating a power level mandatory?
mtg_a2: If the event requires it — yes. Organizers use this data to plan tables. If you are unsure, give an approximate value; that is better than leaving it blank.
mtg_q3: What if I disagree with someone else's power rating?
mtg_a3: Power ratings are subjective, and that is fine. Organizers can facilitate a pre-game talk at the table — a standard Commander practice. The goal is not precision but avoiding a precon-versus-cEDH mismatch.
mtg_q4: Is cEDH a separate format or bracket 5?
mtg_a4: Bracket 5. The rules and card pool are the same as Commander — the difference is optimization and a competitive mindset. There is no separate cEDH format in MTGx.
mtg_q5: What does the proxy policy on an event mean?
mtg_a5: Three levels exist: "No proxies" means originals only; "Partial" means at the organizer's discretion (check the description); "Fully allowed" means any proxies are welcome. If a policy says "Partial", ask the organizer before you arrive.
mtg_a6: Each event is tagged by the organizer: Casual / Competitive / Deck testing / Learning. This sets expectations about the atmosphere before you register.
mtg_a7: Pauper, Commander, Standard, and Draft. The selection is based on activity in the Israeli community. New formats such as Pioneer and Modern will be added as demand grows.
mtg_a8: Your profile has an "Interested in trading" flag. Other players can see this and arrange trades in person at an event. There is no full marketplace or trade list yet.
mtg_q9: Who pays for boosters in Draft?
mtg_a9: MTGx does not process payments. Organizers specify the conditions in the event description — for example "boosters included in the entry fee" or "each player brings 3 boosters". Payment happens offline.
mtg_q10: What is the reliability rating and how is it calculated?
mtg_a10: It is calculated automatically: how many times you RSVPed and actually showed up (based on QR check-in). It is visible only to organizers. There are no penalties — it is purely information for planning. Cancelling your RSVP in advance does not affect it negatively.
mtg_q11: Why is the reliability rating visible only to organizers?
mtg_a11: It is a deliberate choice. A public rating in a small community quickly becomes a tool for social pressure. Organizers need it for table planning, and that is sufficient.
mtg_q12: Can I add a card trade list to my profile?
mtg_a12: A full trade list feature is not available yet. For now, enable the "Interested in trading" flag in your profile so other players know to approach you at events.
clubs_q1: What does a club page in MTGx offer?
clubs_a1: A club page includes a logo, description, photo gallery, contacts (website, phone, WhatsApp, Facebook, Instagram), a "How to find us" section with a map and Google Maps / Waze buttons, directions, and an entrance photo. All club events are also shown on one page.
clubs_q2: Why do I need a page in MTGx if I already have Instagram?
clubs_a2: Instagram attracts new customers. Your MTGx page is for players who are right now looking for a place to play. A player opens the club map, finds you, gets directions in Waze — and walks in.
clubs_q3: How do I find a nearby club?
clubs_a3: Open the "Clubs" section in the bottom navigation to see the club map. It automatically shows the nearest clubs based on your location. Tap a marker to open the club page.
clubs_a4: Yes — club owners can see unique players per month, popular formats, and attendance figures. This is data that clubs previously had no way to access.
notifications_a1: Three types: subscribe to an organizer, to a club, or to a format+city combination. Use the "Subscribe" button on organizer and club pages. You receive one notification per event with no duplicates.
notifications_q2: I am getting too many notifications — how do I reduce them?
notifications_a2: Go to Profile settings and find the Notifications section. You can disable specific notification types, limit the number per day, or set a "Do not disturb" timer.
notifications_q3: I only want to know about Commander in Tel Aviv — how do I set that up?
notifications_a3: Create a format+city subscription: Commander + Tel Aviv. You will only receive notifications about new events matching those parameters.
notifications_a4: In your privacy settings you can choose: everyone, only players you have played with before, only members of your clubs, or nobody.
privacy_q1: What do other players see in my profile?
privacy_a1: Your name, avatar, city, and formats are visible to others. Your email, WhatsApp number, and reliability rating are not shown to other players.
privacy_q2: Who can see my WhatsApp number?
privacy_a2: Only organizers of events you have registered for. Providing your number is optional.
privacy_q3: Can I hide my profile completely?
privacy_a3: Full profile hiding is not available — MTGx is a social platform. However, you control what you share: optional fields such as WhatsApp, car availability, and trade interest do not have to be filled in.
privacy_q4: How do I delete my account?
privacy_a4: Use the "Report a problem" button and submit a deletion request. Your profile, RSVP history, and all associated data will be removed.
privacy_a5: Your data is stored in a secure cloud infrastructure (Supabase). Each user can only access what they are permitted to see. No passwords are stored — authentication is handled by Google.
tech_support_q1: Why is MTGx not in the App Store or Google Play?
tech_support_a1: MTGx is a web application (PWA). Open mtgx.app in your browser and everything works. You can add it to your home screen for convenience. A native app is planned for the future.
tech_support_q2: How do I add MTGx to my home screen?
tech_support_a2: On Android (Chrome): tap the three-dot menu and select "Add to Home screen". On iPhone (Safari): tap the Share button and then "Add to Home Screen". Important: on iPhone you must use Safari.
tech_support_q3: Why should I add it to my home screen?
tech_support_a3: Two benefits: convenience (opens with one tap) and push notifications. On iPhone, push notifications only work if the app has been added to the home screen.
tech_support_q4: Push notifications are not arriving. What should I do?
tech_support_a4: Check the following: 1) Notifications for the browser are allowed in your phone settings. 2) On iPhone — did you add MTGx to your home screen via Safari? 3) iOS must be version 16.4 or later. 4) Make sure Do Not Disturb is not enabled.
tech_support_q5: The app shows a white screen.
tech_support_a5: Refresh the page. If that does not help, clear your browser cache and reopen mtgx.app. If the problem persists, contact support and include your phone model and browser.
tech_support_q6: The app was working and then stopped.
tech_support_a6: Most likely a cache conflict caused by an update. Close MTGx, wait 10 seconds, and reopen it. If that does not help, clear your browser cache.
tech_support_q7: What devices does it work on?
tech_support_a7: Android (Chrome recommended), iPhone (Safari required for push notifications), and desktop (any browser). The only requirement is a modern, up-to-date browser.
tech_support_a8: Partially. Previously loaded data is available from the cache. However, RSVPing, LFG, and creating events all require an internet connection.
tech_support_q9: I have two Google accounts — which one should I use?
tech_support_a9: Your browser will show a list of accounts when you sign in — just pick the one you want. If you signed in with the wrong account, go to Profile, tap "Sign out", and sign in again.
tech_support_q10: Something is not working or I have an idea — where do I write?
tech_support_a10: The "Report a problem" button is available from any screen. Bugs, suggestions, and questions all go directly to the team.
responsibility_q1: Does MTGx process payments for tournaments?
responsibility_a1: No. MTGx is a coordination platform, not a payment processor. Entry fees, prizes, and booster payments are handled directly between organizers and players. MTGx bears no responsibility for financial arrangements.
responsibility_q2: An organizer created an event, gathered players, and never showed up. What can I do?
responsibility_a2: Use "Report a problem" — this automatically affects the organizer's reliability rating. Organizers also have a reputation visible to players: number of events held, cancellation rate, and average attendance.
responsibility_q3: My child wants to use MTGx. Is it safe?
responsibility_a3: Registration requires a Google account (minimum age 13 per Google policy). MTGx does not verify age. We recommend parents choose events at public venues (clubs, game stores) and accompany children at first meetups.
responsibility_q4: Does MTGx replace WPN tools (EventLink, Companion)?
responsibility_a4: No, it complements them. WPN stores continue to use EventLink for official tournaments. MTGx fills the gap: casual games, home Commander sessions, and spontaneous meetups.
